items.title: Docker Postgres is healthy on host port 8765
items.expected: Container health is 'healthy' and TCP port 8765 accepts connections.
items.evidence: Health 'healthy' on attempt 1; 'Connection to localhost port 8765 succeeded'.

items.title: Migrations create all four tables with indexes
steps: 2. docker exec pm_tool_postgres psql -U pmtool -d pm_tool -c "\dt"
3. Query pg_indexes for the four tables
items.expected: interview_sessions, interview_turns, decision_record, tickets exist; indexes on owner_user_id+status and each session_id; unique(session_id,turn_index).
items.evidence: 6 tables (4 app + 2 knex_*); 9 indexes incl. interview_sessions_owner_user_id_status_index and *_session_id_index on all children.

items.title: knex migrate:rollback reverses cleanly, then re-applies
items.expected: Rollback drops all four app tables; re-apply restores them.
items.evidence: After rollback only knex_migrations + knex_migrations_lock remain; re-apply restored 4 app tables.

items.title: GET /health returns the success envelope only when the DB responds
items.expected: 200 with { success:true, data:{ status:'ok', database:'up' }, error:null }.
items.evidence: 200; body {"success":true,"data":{"status":"ok","database":"up","timestamp":"..."},"error":null}. Down-path covered structurally (HealthModel.ping throws -> 503) + HealthController unit test.

items.title: POST /sessions creates a row and returns 201 + envelope
items.precondition: API running; owner via x-dev-user-id header (server-side).
items.expected: 201; { success:true, data:{ id, owner_user_id:5001, status:'draft', original_request } }; row present in DB.
items.evidence: 201; data.id=33, owner_user_id=5001, status=draft; psql confirmed the row.

items.title: POST then GET /sessions/:id round-trips the persisted row
items.evidence: 200; body contained 'Integration: add a CSV export button.'

items.title: Owner scope: a different owner cannot read the session (§11.7)
items.expected: 404 with error.code SESSION_NOT_FOUND — no leak of another owner's row.
items.evidence: 404; error.code SESSION_NOT_FOUND. Also proven by InterviewSessionModel.test.ts isolation test.

items.title: Validation at the boundary: empty request -> 400, bad id -> 400
items.evidence: Empty body -> 400 (curl + supertest, code VALIDATION_ERROR); non-numeric id -> 400 (supertest).

items.title: npx tsc --noEmit is clean on both src/ and frontend/
items.expected: Zero TypeScript errors on both trees.
items.evidence: Backend: 'BACKEND TSC CLEAN'. Frontend: app + node projects both clean.

items.title: npm run check:conventions --strict passes (0 backend violations)
items.expected: Exit 0; 0 backend hard violations, 0 advisory across both trees.
items.evidence: Exit 0; 'Backend hard violations: 0', 'Advisory findings: 0'. Checker verified to still catch a real db()/axios violation via probe.

items.title: Automated tests are green (Layer 1, §20.1-§20.2)
items.precondition: DB up for backend integration tests.
items.expected: Backend 13/13 pass (contract, error paths, owner-scope, JSONB round-trip); frontend 2/2 pass (envelope contract).
items.evidence: Backend: Test Files 4 passed, Tests 13 passed. Frontend: Test Files 1 passed, Tests 2 passed.

items.title: Lint clean on both trees; frontend production build succeeds
items.expected: ESLint clean both trees; vite build produces dist/.
items.evidence: Both lints clean. Build: 137 modules transformed, dist/assets emitted (~97KB gzip JS).

items.title: Wizard step 1 posts to /sessions and advances (manual UI)
items.precondition: Backend on :4000 and `cd frontend && npm run dev` on :5173.
steps: 2. Type a request in the step-1 textarea
items.expected: POST /sessions fires; the wizard advances to step 2 and shows 'Session #N created and saved' echoing the request.
items.evidence: Driven in a real browser via the preview tool: filled the textarea, clicked 'Start interview'. Network shows POST /api/sessions -> 201 (envelope data.id=43, owner_user_id=1 from server context). UI advanced to step 2 'Interview', rendered 'Session #43 created and saved' and echoed the request text. Screenshots captured both steps.
items.notes: API + Vite dev server launched via .claude/launch.json; proxy /api -> :4000.
